notes: XTX Markets is a London-based quantitative trading firm. Kajaani also hosts CSC's LUMI supercomputer. Location chosen for cold climate and renewable energy. Phase 1 first data center connects 22MW by 2026, four more buildings to follow.

Cooler Finnish climate reduces cooling costs vs Germany. 100% renewable energy (hydro + wind). 30min from Helsinki, 15min from Vantaa airport.
notes: German company expanding aggressively in Finland due to cooler climate and lower electricity prices. Large campus with ongoing expansion. Limited technical cooling specs published publicly â€” opportunity for cooling vendors to engage.

opportunity_summary: Google Hamina is a landmark seawater-cooled hyperscale DC built in a former paper mill since 2009. Uses Gulf of Finland seawater pumped through pipes up to 2m diameter, via renovated 20-year-old pumps. Water-to-water heat exchange modules. EUR 3.5B invested to date. 7th DC under construction. Heat recovery with Haminan Energia supplies ~80% of city heating via 7.5MW heat pump. Pioneering seawater cooling design.
notes: Built in former Summa paper mill. Pioneering seawater cooling design. Return water mixed with incoming seawater before discharge to minimize thermal impact. Haminan Energia heat pump extracts ~80% of Hamina city's district heating needs from DC waste heat. EUR 3.5B cumulative investment makes this one of Finland's largest single-site investments.

Waste heat used to heat neighbourhood households via district heating. Elisa is a major Finnish telecom operator â€” potential telecom-to-AI conversion opportunity.

Air cool chiller technology, no freshwater cooling required. Exploring heat reuse. Site demolition Q3 2025, Phase 1 ready 2027. Also developing projects in Kouvola (with Hyperco) and NurmijÃ¤rvi.
notes: Singapore-based hyperscale developer entering Finnish market. Also developing Kouvola site (with local partner Hyperco) and NurmijÃ¤rvi (near Helsinki). EUR 1.2B single investment shows confidence in Finland as DC hub.

opportunity_summary: Interxion CPH3 is Digital Realty's third Copenhagen data center. 14MW at full build-out. 26,968 sqm (75,000 sqft). 2N+1 cooling redundancy. Free cooling, ground water cooling, and waste heat reuse. Third data center on Copenhagen campus alongside CPH1 (28,000 sqm) and CPH2 (27,200 sqm).
notes: Interxion rebranded to Digital Realty in 2022. CPH campus includes CPH1 and CPH2 (both with 2N+1 cooling). Committed to free cooling, ground water cooling, and waste heat reuse across Danish operations.

Air-cooled turbomiser chillers with oil-free centrifugal compressors (241 kW cooling capacity each unit). Carrier-neutral connectivity.
notes: GlobalConnect is a major Nordic telecom and connectivity provider. 27,000 sqm total DC space across Nordics and N. Germany. Limited public technical specs.

opportunity_summary: Colt DCS London 3 (London North) in Welwyn Garden City. PUE 1.15 via distributed cooling system. 33 MVA on-site power. 8kW per rack. Tier III minimum with N+N power design. 20 miles north of London. Recently added 4 new data halls.
notes: Colt DCS is owned by Fidelity Investments. London 3 is north campus. 8kW/rack is relatively low density â€” potential for DLC upgrade if AI workloads increase. Managed by Dalkia UK for technical facilities.

BREEAM Very Good target. 100% renewable energy PPA. Waste heat recovery and water recycling planned. Phase 1 of larger Hayes campus expanding to 160MW (London 4, 6, 7, 8). GBP 2.5B total investment. London 6/7/8 add 97MW, construction mid-2026, L6 live early 2029.
notes: One of the largest DC investments in UK AI economy. London 4 is first phase; London 6/7/8 approved for additional 97MW. Green walls and screening panels. Smart building controls for cooling efficiency optimization. Cundall is engineering consultant.

notes: Part of the larger Telehouse London Docklands campus (North, East, West buildings). World's first multi-storey indirect adiabatic cooling system is a notable engineering achievement. KDDI-owned. Telehouse West Two (33MW, GBP 275M) broke ground Oct 2025 for 2028 completion.

opportunity_summary: DataVita DV1 in North Lanarkshire is Scotland's largest data center. Current 40MW capacity with 500MW+ expansion planned. Supports direct-to-chip, air, and immersive cooling. Adiabatic cooling (Scotland avg 8C year-round eliminates mechanical cooling need). Design PUE 1.18 full load; new expansion targets 1.15. Closed-loop cooling, near-zero water consumption. UK AI Growth Zone designation. 35% lower operating costs than other European hubs.
notes: DataVita also operates DV2 in Glasgow city center (177 Bothwell Street). Scotland's cool climate (avg 8C) is a key competitive advantage. North Lanarkshire site named UK AI Growth Zone. Modular designs for rapid deployment (4-6 weeks). 35% cost advantage vs other EU hubs due to renewable energy and climate.

Low energy indirect evaporative cooling with borehole water extraction. Major financial and enterprise connectivity hub. Part of extensive London LD series (LD1-LD13+).
notes: Part of Equinix's extensive London portfolio (LD1-LD13+). LD8 is a major peering/connectivity hub in Docklands. Borehole water cooling is notable. Equinix rolling out liquid cooling across portfolio â€” LD8 is a potential retrofit candidate.

EUR 600M debt financing secured. Ground broken September 2025, first DC completion early 2027. Up to 9 buildings planned. 100% renewable energy. District heating integration can offset up to 25% of cooling spend. EcoDataCenter offers air, DLC, and immersion cooling across portfolio.
notes: Former Stora Enso papermill site in Kvarnsveden. Borlange Energi stated site could reach 360MW. EcoDataCenter renderings show up to 9 buildings. District heating PPA structured to reward both renewable sourcing and heat sales â€” up to 25% cooling cost offset. Same parent as Falun and Pitea sites.

Liquid cooling system sends excess heat to district heating for surrounding 600,000 sqm business park. Closed-loop: heated liquid heats buildings, cools in transit, returns to DC. Designed to power cities up to 18,000 people. 40% overall energy reduction. Solar-powered buildings feed energy back. 3,000-5,000 new houses planned alongside.
notes: Innovative concept: world's first energy-positive city with DC at center. Snohetta is internationally renowned architecture firm. Bjornafjorden municipality developing Lyseparken with infrastructure for large-scale DC heat recycling from day one. Original target date was 2021 â€” current status needs verification. Small 2MW facility but high innovation value.

opportunity_summary: Small edge DC in Panama City. 1.5MW initial, 5MW max. 160 racks. Part of Telconet's LATAM expansion (8 DCs planned). $10M+ investment. East-coast submarine cable connectivity. Tropical climate = cooling opportunity. Small and new = good pilot candidate.

facility_name: Cerebras x Guyana AI Data Center — Wales
opportunity_summary: Cerebras CS-3 (WSE-3) is air-cooled by design — unique exception in AI compute. 100MW facility in Guyana near gas plant. MoU stage only. Cerebras architecture reduces liquid cooling need vs GPU competitors. However at 100MW scale, facility-level cooling still significant. Caribbean climate makes cooling infrastructure critical.

facility_name: Raxio Tanzania TZ1 — Dar es Salaam
opportunity_summary: Tanzania's first carrier-neutral Tier III DC. 6MW, 800 racks, indirect adiabatic cooling (PUE 1.3). IFC-backed Raxio Group ($100M commitment to African DCs). 2N power, 48hr fuel backup. 11 carriers. Under construction for 2026 launch. Hot climate = cooling is critical infrastructure. Future high-density or AI workloads could drive liquid cooling upgrade.

Rwanda's carrier-neutral DC at Kigali Innovation City. 3MW power, ISO 27001 + PCI DSS. 10km from airport. Small (1300 sqft raised floor). Modern design with glass/steel/local stone. East Africa tech hub location. Very small = ideal pilot. Kigali at 1,567m altitude = naturally cooler.

facility_name: Data Vault x Telenor Pakistan AI DC — Karachi
opportunity_summary: Pakistan's first AI DC with 3,000+ NVIDIA H100/H200 GPUs. Solar-powered. GPU-as-a-service model. Hopper architecture at this density benefits significantly from liquid cooling (40-80 kW/rack range). Karachi = hot climate, making cooling critical. Solar power = sustainability signal. If expanding to Blackwell, liquid cooling becomes mandatory.

Pakistani oil company diversifying into DC infrastructure. 5MW Tier III/IV in Islamabad with $36M investment. Early 2026 completion. Second facility in Karachi. Oil & gas parent company = capital and energy expertise. New entrant to DC market = cooling vendor could win design-stage influence. Islamabad = moderate climate.

Bangladesh's first cloud DC. 4MW, Tier IV, 200,000 sqft. $500M 5-year investment plan. Phase 1 operational March 2025. Hot humid climate (Dhaka ~30C avg) = intensive cooling needed. 25 cubic meters cooling water per MW/day. Rapid market growth (45% CAGR to 150.6 MW by 2030). Water scarcity concerns in region could drive liquid cooling adoption.

facility_name: Osiris Group x Jatra Tier IV DC — Kaliakoir, Bangladesh
opportunity_summary: Largest planned DC in Bangladesh. $200M investment, 4,800 racks, 28.8MW IT. Tier IV. Two buildings at Kaliakoir Hi-Tech Park. Under construction. Standard rack density (~6kW) suggests traditional cooling initially. But 28.8MW in hot/humid Bangladesh = significant cooling challenge. Water scarcity in region flagged by environmental journalists.

Google's first Pacific Island DC. FJ$200M investment with 4 subsea cables. Google globally uses liquid cooling for TPU/GPU racks (demonstrated 1MW racks at OCP EMEA). Tropical climate = cooling is critical. No specs disclosed but Google's standard includes advanced cooling. Monitor for design-stage cooling vendor opportunities.

NEXTDC's NZ debut. 15MW (upgraded from 10MW) in Auckland. AU$140M investment. Part of $1.42B international expansion. Phase 1 H1 2026. NEXTDC is Australia's leading DC operator — their expertise includes high-density and liquid cooling from Australian facilities. NZ market growing to 70+ MW operational by end 2026.

facility_name: Spark NZ Dairy Flat DC — Auckland (w/ Surf Park)
opportunity_summary: World-first: DC waste heat warms adjacent surf lagoon. Spark NZ's flagship 40MW campus with 7-hectare solar farm. Resource consent granted. Stage 1 at 10MW. Heat recovery integration = sophisticated thermal management = strong liquid cooling vendor fit. Spark looking for co-investor for DC portfolio. Auckland building to 160+ MW market.

NZ's leading enterprise DC with confirmed DLC (direct liquid cooling) capability. 10MW with multi-MW DLC capacity. Hybrid air-liquid racks. Air-side economizer + chilled water (2N pumping). Spark NZ is a colocation tenant. Datacom has 6 DCs across NZ/AU. Already DLC-ready = cooling vendor partnership opportunity for GPU-specific cooling solutions.

cooling_type: Eviden BullSequana XH3000 direct liquid cooling with warm water; waste heat piped to campus heating
opportunity_summary: Europe's first exascale supercomputer. 1 ExaFLOP/s. #4 TOP500. Most energy-efficient exascale system globally (60 GFlops/watt). #1 Green500 for exascale class. ~24,000 NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips. Eviden BullSequana XH3000 DLC. ~17MW power budget. Waste heat heats campus buildings. EUR 500M total budget.
notes: EUR 500M total budget (273M hardware, 227M operations). Europe's first exascale. Waste heat integrated into Juelich campus heating network. #1 Green500 exascale class.

cooling_type: advanced direct liquid cooling (Eviden BullSequana XH3000 + Lenovo ThinkSystem)
opportunity_summary: 314 PFLOPS peak. 20MW total power, 17MW cooling capacity, PUE below 1.08. 6th greenest supercomputer worldwide (Green500). Eviden BullSequana XH3000 accelerator partition with NVIDIA H100 GPUs + Intel Sapphire Rapids. Advanced DLC on both accelerator and general purpose partitions.
notes: 6th greenest supercomputer globally per Green500. Hybrid architecture: Eviden BullSequana XH3000 (GPU partition) + Lenovo ThinkSystem (general purpose).

cooling_type: liquid circulation cooling with 100% free cooling; waste heat to district heating
opportunity_summary: 380 PFLOPS. HPE Cray EX with AMD MI250X GPUs. PUE 1.03 with 100% free cooling. 100% hydroelectric power. Waste heat provides 20% of Kajaani city district heating — negative CO2 footprint (13,500 tons/year reduced). Greenest supercomputer in Europe.
notes: Median power 3.8MW, max 5.8MW (LUMI-G partition). Nameplate TDP ~8MW. Negative CO2 footprint due to district heating displacement. 200MW grid feed available. Greenest supercomputer in Europe (#3 Green500 globally).

facility_name: Alps — Swiss National Supercomputing Centre (CSCS), Lugano
cooling_type: lake water cooling from Lake Lugano (45m depth, 460 L/sec via 2.8km pipes); waste heat supplies hot water to Lugano
opportunity_summary: TOP500 #7 (Nov 2024). 435 PFLOPS. 10,752 NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips. HPE Cray EX. Lake Lugano water cooling (45m depth, 460 L/s, 2.8km pipes). Waste heat supplies hot water to city of Lugano. 100% hydropower. 10MW at full load.
notes: Unique cooling: Lake Lugano water from 45m depth. Waste heat repurposed for Lugano city hot water. 100% hydropower. Successor to Piz Daint.

cooling_type: warm water cooling (Lenovo Neptune); waste heat reused to heat offices and generate cold water
opportunity_summary: 26.9 PFLOPS. PUE 1.05. Lenovo Neptune warm-water cooling since SuperMUC (2012). Waste heat reused for office heating and cold water generation. Blue Lion successor (2027) will be 100% DLC with 40C warm water, 30x more performance, EUR 250M.
notes: LRZ pioneered warm-water cooling in HPC since 2012 (original SuperMUC). Blue Lion successor (2027, EUR 250M) will use HPE Cray with NVIDIA Vera Rubin architecture, 100% DLC at 40C.

operator_company: University of Bristol / Bristol Centre for Supercomputing
cooling_type: 100% fan-less HPE direct liquid cooling (up to 90% reduction in cooling power consumption)
opportunity_summary: UK's most powerful supercomputer. 21 exaflops AI performance. 5,448 NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips. 100% fan-less HPE DLC with 90% cooling power reduction. 5MW facility. #4 Green500 worldwide. #6 fastest in Europe. Heat recovery pathway for industrial/district heating.
notes: GBP 225M investment. Located at National Composites Centre. Heat recovery pathway enables industrial processes or district heating. #4 Green500 globally. #6 fastest in Europe.

facility_name: ARCHER2 — EPCC Advanced Computing Facility, Edinburgh
cooling_type: water-cooled HPE Cray EX Mountain cabinets with CDU heat exchangers
opportunity_summary: 28 PFLOPS. 23-cabinet HPE Cray EX with water-cooled Mountain cabinets. 5,860 compute nodes, 748,544 AMD EPYC cores. ACF upgraded from 8MW to 38MW capacity in 2023. ~70 GWh/year waste heat released. Mine water heat recovery being explored.
notes: ACF facility upgraded to 38MW capacity. ~70 GWh/year excess heat. EPCC exploring mine water geothermal heat recovery (homes heating). PUE ~1.1 estimated but not officially validated.

operator_company: IDRIS (Institut du Developpement et des Ressources en Informatique Scientifique) / CNRS
cooling_type: warm-water liquid cooling (30-36C, vs 12C on predecessor); waste heat to district heating
opportunity_summary: France's most powerful research supercomputer. 125.9 PFLOPS peak. Warm-water DLC at 30-36C. H100 partition installed by Eviden (April 2024). Waste heat pipes to Paris-Saclay district heating network — heats equivalent of 1,000 homes. ~2MW / 17 GWh/year.
notes: Pioneer in HPC waste heat recovery in France. Pipes connected to EPAPS Paris-Saclay district heating network. Eviden H100 partition added 2024.

cooling_type: direct water cooling (adiabatic cooling of external units); waste heat heats IZUM building
opportunity_summary: First operational EuroHPC system. 6.9 PFLOPS. BullSequana XH2000 with DLC. 830 kW power, cooling requires only 50 kW extra. Annual PUE <1.06. Waste heat heats IZUM building. 960 CPU + 60 GPU nodes (A100).
notes: First operational EuroHPC system. EUR 17.3M contract. Adiabatic cooling of external units. Only 50 kW for cooling at 830 kW IT load.

cooling_type: direct liquid cooling (90% hot water + 10% air, Atos BullSequana XH2000)
opportunity_summary: 4.5 PFLOPS. BullSequana XH2000 with DLC (90% hot water, 10% air). 12 racks, 1,128 compute nodes, 144,384 AMD EPYC cores. 1MW UPS. TOP500 #91. EUR 11.5M EuroHPC co-investment. Major upgrade announced by EuroHPC.
notes: Major upgrade announced by EuroHPC JU. UPS capacity 1MW. 90/10 water/air DLC split.

cooling_type: cold water-cooled racks (8-15C cold water outlets, 30-35C hot water outlets)
opportunity_summary: 15.7 PFLOPS peak. 829 compute nodes, 106,752 cores. 800 kW power. Cold water-cooled racks (8-15C / 30-35C). 72 nodes with NVIDIA A100 GPUs. #15 Green500 at launch. EuroHPC petascale system.
notes: HPE Apollo 2000Gen10 Plus + HPE Apollo 6500. AMD Zen 2/3 + Intel Cascade Lake. 72 nodes with NVIDIA A100.

opportunity_summary: 18 PFLOPS, 20 PB storage. BullSequana XH2000 water-cooled platform. AMD EPYC + NVIDIA A100 GPUs. Powered by green energy from wood waste cogeneration. #4 greenest supercomputer in the EU. 26.957 GFlops/watt.
notes: EUR 30.4M acquisition. Powered by wood waste cogeneration. Named after Luxembourg's legend of mermaid Melusina.

facility_name: Dardel — KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm
operator_company: PDC Center for High Performance Computing, KTH
cooling_type: liquid cooling (CDU-controlled water cooling for HPE Cray EX system)
opportunity_summary: Sweden's fastest supercomputer. 13.5 PFLOPS peak. HPE Cray EX with CDU-controlled liquid cooling. 1,278 CPU nodes (AMD EPYC) + 62 GPU nodes. Most environmentally friendly per calculation in the world.
notes: Sweden's fastest HPC. Swedish grid has high renewable content.

facility_name: Fugaku — RIKEN Center for Computational Science (R-CCS), Kobe
cooling_type: direct liquid cooling (water at ~15C to CPUs, 9:1 water-to-air ratio, Fujikura cold plates, secondary pipe system)
opportunity_summary: Former world #1 supercomputer. 442 PFLOPS. ~160,000 Fujitsu A64FX ARM CPUs. DLC with 9:1 water-to-air ratio. ~15C inlet water, exits 19-25C. ~30MW power (constrained by facility 30MW capacity). 100+ kW/m2 heat density. Successor announced 2025 with 32C warm water cooling.
notes: Former #1 TOP500 (2020-2022). Fujikura cold plates on CPUs. 100+ kW/m2 heat density. Successor system announced Nov 2025: will use NVIDIA GH200 + Fujitsu Monaka chips with 32C warm water cooling.

operator_company: National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology (AIST)
cooling_type: HPE Cray XD liquid cooling (direct liquid cooling for GPU nodes)
opportunity_summary: 6.2 exaflops AI performance. HPE Cray XD with NVIDIA H200 GPUs (8 per node, NVLink-connected). Quantum-2 InfiniBand at 200 Gb/s. Built for sovereign Japanese AI. Successor to ABCI 2.0.
notes: HPE Cray XD platform implies direct liquid cooling (standard for XD series). ABCI-Q quantum computer being co-located. Japanese sovereign AI initiative.

facility_name: Sunway TaihuLight — National Supercomputing Center, Wuxi
cooling_type: closed-coupled chilled water cooling (Climaveneta 28MW cooling, 15 watercooled chillers with magnetic levitation compressors)
opportunity_summary: 93 PFLOPS. Former #1 TOP500 (2016-2018). 15.37 MW average power. 28 MW Climaveneta cooling system with 15 watercooled chillers (magnetic levitation VFD compressors). Domestic Sunway SW26010 processors. TOP500 #24 as of Nov 2025.
notes: Domestic Chinese processors (Sunway SW26010, 260 cores each). Climaveneta (Mitsubishi-owned) cooling. 6 GFlops/watt efficiency.

cooling_type: chilled water cooling (customized liquid water cooling unit; 80kW cooling capacity; city water source)
opportunity_summary: 33.86 PFLOPS. Former #1 TOP500 (2013-2015). 17.6 MW IT load, 24 MW with cooling. Customized chilled water cooling. Located in Guangzhou University Town with district cooling. Intel Xeon + Matrix-2000 accelerators.
notes: Located at Guangzhou University Town with district cooling system. NUDT-developed Matrix-2000 accelerators. Tianhe-3 successor believed operational but not on TOP500.

operator_company: Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC)
cooling_type: liquid cooling (NVIDIA DGX A100 systems with standard DGX cooling infrastructure)
source_1_publisher: Press Information Bureau, Government of India
opportunity_summary: 410 AI PFLOPS combined (AIRAWAT 200 + PARAM Siddhi 210). 8.5 PFLOPS sustained (Rmax). TOP500 #75. NVIDIA DGX A100 systems with AMD EPYC 7742 CPUs + NVIDIA A100 GPUs. India's National Program on AI.
notes: Liquid cooling inferred from NVIDIA DGX A100 standard architecture. Direct cooling infrastructure details limited in public sources. India's flagship AI supercomputer.

facility_name: Deucalion — Minho Advanced Computing Centre (MACC), Guimaraes
cooling_type: liquid cooling (Fujitsu PRIMEHPC FX700 + Atos Bull Sequana partitions)
opportunity_summary: 10 PFLOPS. Unique hybrid: ARM A64FX (Fujitsu) + x86 (Atos Bull Sequana) + GPU (NVIDIA A100) partitions. First EuroHPC system with ARM processors. TOP500 #219. Green500 #80. EUR 20M co-investment. Renewable energy commitment.
notes: First EuroHPC ARM-based supercomputer. Renewable energy from solar/wind planned. Liquid cooling inferred from BullSequana and PRIMEHPC platforms (both use DLC by default).

opportunity_summary: 1GW AI campus. Broke ground Feb 11, 2026. $10B+ investment. 4M sq ft. Gigawatt-scale architecture purpose-built for liquid cooling and high-density AI server racks. Operational late 2027 / early 2028.
notes: Announced Feb 11, 2026. LEAP Research and Innovation District. 4,000 construction jobs, 300 operational. Meta commits to 100% water restoration to local watersheds. Source: Meta Newsroom Feb 2026, Bloomberg Feb 2026.

facility_name: Meta El Paso AI Data Center
opportunity_summary: 1GW AI data center. Investment raised from $1.5B to $10B. Liquid-cooled closed-loop system with zero water for majority of year. 200% water restoration commitment. Groundbreaking Oct 2025, expansion announced Mar 2026.
notes: Broke ground Oct 2025. Mar 2026 expansion to 1GW/$10B announced. El Paso Electric deploying 813 modular generators for power. Online later this decade. Source: CNBC Mar 2026, Meta blog Mar 2026.

facility_name: DarkNX Ontario AI Data Center Campus
opportunity_summary: 300MW campus. Largest two-phase direct-to-chip deployment to date. Accelsius NeuCool technology. Phase 1: two 65MW facilities deploying 2026-2027. Johnson Controls chillers for facility cooling.
notes: Announced Nov 17, 2025. Deployments begin 2026. DarkNX is developer of high-density modular DCs for AI/GPU workloads. Source: BusinessWire Nov 2025, Accelsius press release, DCD confirmation.

facility_name: NJFX Project Cool Water AI Hall
opportunity_summary: 10MW liquid-cooled AI data hall at cable landing station campus. Liquid-to-the-chip infrastructure as standard. PUE target 1.25. CDUs, AFC chillers, hot-aisle containment, fan-wall. Power delivery by end of 2026.
notes: Basis of Design completed. $3M utility deposit for power delivery. First purpose-built AI-ready environment within a North American cable landing station. 8MW usable IT load. Source: DCD 2026, NJFX press release, Data Centre Magazine.

source_1_publisher: West Virginia Division of Economic Development
opportunity_summary: 600MW IT capacity on 548 acres. $4B investment. 1.9M sq ft at full build-out. Water-based cooling system planned. Planning commission approved water-based cooling. Reclaimed water opportunities identified.
notes: Announced Feb 2026. 1,000 construction jobs, 125 permanent. One of largest digital infrastructure investments ever in WV. Source: WV State Gov Feb 2026, DCD, Construction Dive.

facility_name: LG CNS Jakarta AI Data Center
opportunity_summary: Hyperscale AI DC. 100,000+ servers. 46,281 sqm. LG Electronics supplying hybrid air+liquid cooling (LeviTech magnetic bearing chillers, CRAHs, AHUs, CRACs, fan walls). Completion scheduled 2026, operations H2 2026.
notes: Contract value ~100B won ($72M). JV between LG CNS and Kuningan Mas Gemilang (Sinar Mas). LG Electronics is cooling vendor. Source: KED Global, Nasdaq, DCD, Korea Times Mar 2026.

opportunity_summary: $4.75B acquisition of CoolIT Systems (direct-to-chip liquid cooling leader). CoolIT generates ~$550M/yr. Closing expected Q3 2026. CoolIT has 5M+ cold plates shipped, 300+ DCs served.
notes: Announced Mar 19-20, 2026. Acquired from KKR. Salute+Ecolab also announced DTC Cooling-as-a-Service collaboration Mar 23, 2026. Source: BusinessWire, Ecolab investor relations, DCD.

opportunity_summary: $9.5B acquisition completed Mar 12, 2026. Boyd Thermal has $1.7B forecast 2026 sales, $1.5B in liquid cooling. Creates grid-to-chip integrated solution. Boyd Juarez MX plant expanding to 460K sqft (doubled).
notes: Completed Mar 12, 2026. Boyd Thermal acquired from Goldman Sachs AM. 22.5x estimated 2026 EBITDA. Boyd Juarez expansion announced Feb 17, 2026. Source: Eaton press release, BusinessWire, DCD.

opportunity_summary: Acquisition announced Feb 10, 2026, now completed. LiquidStack provides direct-to-chip and immersion cooling. Operations in Texas and Hong Kong. Builds on Trane's 2023 minority investment.
notes: Announced Feb 10, 2026. Completed (date confirmed on Trane IR page). LiquidStack supports racks 50kW-100kW+. Financial terms not disclosed. Source: Trane IR, The Register Feb 2026, Facilities Dive.

opportunity_summary: $50M investment to expand Ironton OH and Westerville OH facilities. 45% capacity increase for liquid cooling and chilled water systems. Hundreds of new jobs through 2029. Operational Q2 2027.
notes: Announced Mar 30, 2026. Also completed $1B PurgeRite acquisition (Dec 2025) for fluid management services supporting liquid cooling deployments. Source: PRNewswire Mar 2026, HPCwire.

opportunity_summary: Major expansion of Juarez thermal campus from 217K to 460K sqft (more than doubled). Supports AI, hyperscale, and colo DC liquid cooling production. Announced Feb 17, 2026.
notes: Boyd Thermal was subsequently acquired by Eaton for $9.5B (completed Mar 12, 2026). Juarez expansion is for liquid cooling component manufacturing at scale. Source: Data Center Frontier Feb 2026.

opportunity_summary: Industrial firm enters DC market. FreeWaterLoop: liquid-based external facility cooling using natural water sources. Integrates pumps, heat exchangers, filtration. Smaller footprint than air-based systems. Launched Mar 4, 2026 at Data Centre World London.
notes: New market entrant — Alfa Laval is major industrial heat exchanger company. FreeWaterLoop minimizes net water consumption, returns water to origin. Source: Alfa Laval press release, PRNewswire, DCD Mar 2026.

opportunity_summary: Panasonic launches CDUs (400kW, 800kW) and free-cooling chillers (800kW, 1200kW) for AI DC liquid cooling in Europe. Orders accepted from Mar 4, 2026. 1200kW+ CDUs coming. Available Autumn 2026.
notes: Panasonic acquired Tecnair S.p.A. (Italy) in 2023 for air cooling. Now expanding into liquid cooling CDUs for generative AI DCs. Source: Panasonic Newsroom Mar 2026, DCD.

facility_name: Infinium Edge Immersion Cooling Platform Launch
opportunity_summary: Low-carbon synthetic dielectric immersion fluid for AI/HPC data centers. Cleaner than petroleum-derived fluids. Launched Jan 15, 2026. Integrates chemistry, process engineering, and scalable manufacturing.
notes: Infinium is primarily a synthetic low-carbon fuel company. Infinium Edge is new immersion cooling fluid product line. Source: PRNewswire Jan 2026, DCD, HPCwire.

facility_name: Frore Systems LiquidJet / LiquidJet Nexus (Series D)
opportunity_summary: $143M Series D at $1.64B valuation (Mar 16, 2026). Total raised $340M. LiquidJet Nexus for 1/2U AI servers + Nvidia Kyber. 3D jetchannel microstructure: 50% higher cooling vs current coldplates. 75% performance increase / 7.7C lower GPU temps.
notes: Manufactures in Taiwan, expanding to additional locations. Led by MVP Ventures with Fidelity, Qualcomm Ventures participation. Source: Frore Systems press release, DCD, SiliconANGLE Mar 2026.

facility_name: Aligned Data Centers - DeltaFlow Liquid Cooling Deployments
opportunity_summary: DeltaFlow liquid cooling + Delta3 air cooling across Dallas TX, Phoenix AZ, Northern VA campuses. Supports 3-300+ kW/rack. $2.58B credit facility secured 2026 for expansion. Partners include Lambda for next-gen AI infra.
notes: DeltaFlow uses facility chilled water for isolated propylene glycol supply. Closed-loop, waterless design. Hybrid air+liquid supported. Source: Aligned DC website, GlobeNewswire, DCN Magazine.

city: Gainesville VA / Chicago IL / Sacramento CA
opportunity_summary: Nearly 115MW combined capacity commitments across Gainesville VA, Chicago IL, Sacramento CA. Announced in April 2026 DCK roundup. Liquid cooling status unconfirmed but NTT supports liquid-ready designs.
notes: Liquid cooling not explicitly confirmed for these specific sites. Included because NTT Data is building liquid-ready facilities as standard in 2026. Source: DCK April 2026 roundup.

facility_name: China Mobile Hohhot Intelligent Computing Center
cooling_type: Cold plate liquid cooling + indirect evaporative cooling + magnetic levitation AC
opportunity_summary: World's largest single-unit liquid-cooled intelligent computing center operated by a telecom. 6.7 EFLOPS capacity, ~20,000 AI accelerator cards. Cold plate liquid cooling at scale with PUE below 1.15. Opened April 2024. Part of China's Eastern Data Western Computing strategy. Annual electricity savings of 30M kWh across buildings 7 and 9.
notes: PUE reported as both 1.17 and below 1.15 depending on source. 6.7 EFLOPS confirmed. Built with Huawei. 85% domestic AI chips. China's three telcos jointly committed to 50%+ liquid cooling in new projects by 2025 via joint white paper.

facility_name: China Mobile Harbin Intelligent Computing Center
opportunity_summary: Planned companion to Hohhot center. Together with Hohhot and Guiyang centers, will deliver computing power equivalent to ~60,000 AI accelerator cards. Severely cold climate (-30C winters) ideal for free cooling supplemented by liquid cooling.
notes: Announced alongside Hohhot opening. Specific liquid cooling details not yet disclosed. Cold climate region should enable very low PUE.

facility_name: China Mobile Guiyang Intelligent Computing Center
opportunity_summary: Third node in China Mobile's national AI computing triangle (Hohhot-Harbin-Guiyang). Located in Gui'an New Area data center cluster, a key hub in China's Eastern Data Western Computing strategy. Guizhou is a major DC destination with cool highland climate and cheap hydro power.
notes: Guizhou region already hosts Tencent, Huawei, Apple data centers. Specific liquid cooling plans for this facility not yet publicly detailed.

facility_name: China Telecom Inner Mongolia Information Park
cooling_type: Advanced cooling (liquid cooling adoption under joint telco white paper commitment)
opportunity_summary: 150MW telecom hyperscale campus in Horinger County south of Hohhot. 994,062 sqm site. Part of China's 8 national data center clusters. China Telecom committed via joint white paper (2023) to deploy liquid cooling in 50%+ of new projects by 2025. Exact liquid cooling status at this specific site not confirmed.
notes: 150MW capacity confirmed by Baxtel. China Telecom is one of three telcos that signed the joint liquid cooling commitment. Massive campus but liquid cooling verification needed for this specific site. Construction began 2011, expanded in phases.

facility_name: China Unicom Xining AI Computing Center
cooling_type: Advanced cooling (liquid cooling adoption under joint telco white paper commitment)
opportunity_summary: ~$390M investment. 3,579 PFLOPS currently (target 20,000 PFLOPS). Uses 23,000 domestically made AI chips (Alibaba and others). Located in Qinghai province at high altitude with cold climate. China Unicom committed via joint white paper to 50%+ liquid cooling in new projects by 2025.
notes: Massive domestic AI chip deployment. Specific liquid cooling implementation at this site not confirmed, but China Unicom's joint commitment to liquid cooling applies to new AI facilities.

facility_name: KDDI Oyama Immersion Cooling Data Center
opportunity_summary: PUE 1.05 achieved via single-phase immersion cooling in container-type modular data center. 94% reduction in cooling energy vs air cooling. Uses GIGABYTE R282-Z93 and R182-Z91 rack servers. 50kVA IT power, 53.5kVA total. Developed with KDDI, MHI (Mitsubishi Heavy Industries), and NESIC. Commercialization targeted FY2024. Carbon neutrality target by FY2026.
notes: PUE 1.05 is among the lowest reported for any telecom DC worldwide. Container-type design enables rapid edge deployment. KDDI aims for carbon-neutral DCs by FY2026.

opportunity_summary: 30MW IT load, 10,900 sqm server room. Liquid cooling installed as standard (Green Nexcenter). pPUE 1.15 target. Opening March 2026. NTT also operates a 75kW 'Data Center Trial Field' in Noda City, Chiba for liquid cooling verification (direct liquid cooling + immersion cooling devices). NTT testing two-phase direct-to-chip cooling in existing air-cooled DC.
notes: pPUE 1.15 is partial PUE (facility-level). 30MW confirmed. Liquid cooling as standard is significant for a telecom-group DC. Trial field in Noda City demonstrates both DLC and immersion.

facility_name: NTT DATA Noda Liquid Cooling Trial Field
cooling_type: Direct liquid cooling + immersion cooling (test facility)
opportunity_summary: 105 sqm verification facility opened November 2024 for liquid cooling R&D. Tests both direct liquid cooling and immersion cooling devices. Built by Hibiya Engineering with Kuwana Metals Chilled Tower cooling system. Purpose: verify optimal cooling water temps/flow rates, monitoring systems, staff training. Feeds into NTT DATA's broader liquid cooling deployment.
notes: Small test facility (75kW at opening) but strategically important as NTT DATA's formal liquid cooling R&D center. Opened November 2024.

cooling_type: Direct liquid cooling + immersion cooling + precision liquid cooling (all three)
opportunity_summary: Korea's first and only facility to deploy all three liquid cooling types simultaneously: DLC, immersion, and precision liquid cooling. Features NVIDIA chips, SK Hynix HBM, GPU virtualization. Opened December 2024. SKT achieved 93% reduction in cooling power vs air cooling in immersion tests. Partnership with Giga Computing and SK Enmove for cooling solutions.
notes: Testbed but operationally significant. SKT planning gigawatt-scale AIDC expansion in Ulsan and other regions. 37% overall power consumption reduction demonstrated.

facility_name: SK Telecom Gasan AI Data Center
cooling_type: Liquid cooling (AI-optimized, details not fully disclosed)
opportunity_summary: Launched December 2024 with Lambda partnership. Features NVIDIA H100 GPUs, planned H200 upgrade (March 2025). Part of SKT's 'AI Infrastructure Superhighway' strategy. Liquid cooling systems deployed for AI workloads. SKT offers GPUaaS (GPU-as-a-Service) from this facility.
notes: Liquid cooling confirmed as part of AI-optimized design. Exact cooling type (DLC vs immersion) not specified for this specific site. Part of broader SKT AIDC network.

facility_name: SK Telecom Ulsan AI Data Center
cooling_type: Liquid cooling (planned, part of GW-scale build)
opportunity_summary: Planned expansion to 1GW-scale capacity. 69,300 sqm complex described as Korea's most advanced AI-optimized data center featuring liquid cooling systems and AI-driven power management. Part of SKT's three-region AIDC strategy (Seoul metro, southern, southwestern Korea). CEO Jung Jaihun's flagship project.
notes: GW-scale ambition announced at SK AI Summit 2025. Liquid cooling confirmed in facility description. Exact timeline and cooling vendor details TBD.

cooling_type: LNG cold energy cooling (waste cold from regasification at -160C)
opportunity_summary: World's first data center cooled by LNG cold energy. Partnership between KT and KOGAS (Korea Gas Corporation). LNG regasification releases cold at -160C. Could save ~12MW of cooling power for a mid-sized DC. 60% reduction in annual power consumption estimated. Located at KOGAS LNG import terminal at Incheon near Seoul.
notes: Novel cooling approach using waste cold energy. Currently in feasibility review and safety verification phase. Not liquid cooling in traditional DC sense but innovative thermal management. If successful, could be replicated at LNG terminals globally.

cooling_type: Direct-to-chip liquid cooling (Singapore's largest DLC deployment)

opportunity_summary: One of Alibaba's five Super Data Centers (with Hangzhou, Zhangbei, Ulanqab, Nantong). Part of the world's largest liquid-cooled data center network. Serves Double 11 (Singles Day) computing peaks. Alibaba's immersion cooling technology deployed across Super DC fleet.
notes: Heyuan in Guangdong province. Part of Alibaba's five Super DC network that collectively form the world's largest liquid-cooled DC cluster. Specific immersion cooling details for Heyuan not individually disclosed but confirmed as part of fleet. Source: Alibaba Cloud Blog.

opportunity_summary: ByteDance self-built DC in Huailai, Hebei Province (near Beijing). Supports Doubao AI model training. Kilocalorie-level GPU clusters requiring liquid cooling as standard (not optional). Guangzhou Goaland Energy Conservation provides liquid cooling solutions to ByteDance. Cold plate = 95% of China's DC liquid cooling market. ByteDance was NVIDIA's largest Chinese customer in 2024.
notes: Huailai is a major DC cluster zone near Beijing. ByteDance operates as 'landlord' of own DCs. Liquid cooling confirmed via Goaland supplier relationship and industry reporting on ByteDance GPU clusters. Source: Tom's Hardware 2025, 36Kr.

opportunity_summary: $2.1B+ Malaysia investment. Working with Aolani Cloud to deploy ~500 NVIDIA Blackwell computing systems (~36,000 B200 chips) in Malaysia. Circumvents US export restrictions by deploying in third country. NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 racks require direct-to-chip liquid cooling. Johor is emerging as major APAC AI hub.
notes: Mar 2026 announcement. NVIDIA confirmed no objections to deal. 36,000 B200 chips = massive liquid cooling requirement. Blackwell architecture requires liquid cooling at rack level. Part of ByteDance's broader $2.1B Malaysia expansion. Source: Seoul Economic Daily Mar 2026, Tom's Hardware Mar 2026.

opportunity_summary: $3.8B investment announced Jan 2025 by Thailand Board of Investment. Operations expected 2026. At this scale and for AI workloads, liquid cooling is extremely likely. Tropical climate = cooling is critical design constraint.
notes: Liquid cooling not individually confirmed but at $3.8B scale with AI workloads in tropical climate, advanced cooling is a near-certainty. Source: DataGarda Jan 2025.

facility_name: Apple Private Cloud Compute — US Data Centers (Multiple Sites)
opportunity_summary: Apple shipping Private Cloud Compute servers from Houston manufacturing facility to DCs across US. M5 chip-based architecture (skipping M3/M4 Ultra). Dedicated AI server chip mass production H2 2026. Apple M-series silicon has much lower TDP than NVIDIA GPUs, so liquid cooling may not be required. Maiden NC uses outside air + waterside economizer. No public disclosure of liquid cooling at Apple DCs.
notes: IMPORTANT: Apple has NOT publicly disclosed liquid cooling at any of its data centers. M-series silicon's low power profile may not require liquid cooling. Maiden uses outside air cooling. Apple's $600B US commitment includes Houston server factory + DC expansion. I could not verify liquid cooling from available sources. Source: Tom's Hardware Oct 2025, 9to5Mac Feb 2026.

facility_name: IBM Quantum Data Center — Ehningen, Germany
opportunity_summary: First IBM Quantum Data Center outside US. Opened Oct 2024. Two IBM Quantum Eagle-based systems + new Heron-based system. Cryogenic cooling to near-absolute-zero + water-cooled support infrastructure. IBM is pioneer of microfluidics and two-phase cooling R&D. Germany is key hub for IBM's water-cooled computing heritage (QPACE supercomputer). IBM now proliferating liquid cooling tech through partners (Microsoft, Google, AWS, Lenovo, AMD, Danfoss).
notes: Quantum DCs use cryogenic dilution refrigerators (15 millikelvin) not conventional liquid cooling. But IBM's broader liquid cooling IP (microfluidics, Corintis partnership with Microsoft) is significant for the DC market. IBM is technology enabler, not competitor to Vertiv etc. Source: IBM Newsroom Oct 2024, EnkiAI 2025.

facility_name: IBM Quantum Computation Center — Poughkeepsie, NY
opportunity_summary: IBM's primary Quantum Computation Center. Houses multiple quantum systems. Project Goldeneye large-scale cryogenic concept system being developed here for next-gen quantum DC cooling needs. IBM's liquid cooling heritage goes back decades (mainframes, POWER systems). Poughkeepsie is IBM's historic computing manufacturing hub.
notes: Cryogenic cooling for quantum + conventional liquid cooling for supporting HPC infrastructure. Project Goldeneye exploring large-scale cryogenic systems for future quantum DCs. Source: IBM Quantum Blog.

facility_name: Compass Datacenters --- Vertiv CoolPhase Flex Deployment
opportunity_summary: First CoolPhase Flex units deployed at Compass facility in Q1 2025. Multi-year, multi-billion dollar supply arrangement. CoolPhase Flex integrates liquid cooling with refrigerant-based air cooling and heat rejection in a single packaged system. Anchor customer for the platform.
notes: Vertiv vendor ecosystem. CoolPhase Flex co-developed with Compass. First deployment Q1 2025. Exact campus not disclosed publicly.

facility_name: Elea Data Centers --- Vertiv Liquid Cooling (Sao Paulo)
opportunity_summary: Hundreds of Vertiv CDUs ordered for Elea's Sao Paulo AI data center sites. Part of $300M first-phase investment. First Latin American commercial DC to adopt liquid-to-chip CDUs. PUE target 1.15-1.2. First phase delivered 2025. Nine sites powered by 100% renewables.
notes: Vertiv vendor ecosystem. Announced Nov 2024. First Vertiv liquid cooling for purpose-built AI DCs in Brazil. Landmark deployment for LATAM region.

facility_name: Schneider Electric / Motivair --- Supercomputer Liquid Cooling (HPC)
opportunity_summary: Motivair CDU technology enables thermal performance for 6 of the world's top 10 supercomputers. Certified for NVIDIA hardware. Schneider acquired controlling interest in Motivair Feb 2025. Fourth production facility opened in Buffalo, NY (Jun 2025). Also expanding in Italy and India. Tripling global manufacturing capacity.
notes: Schneider Electric vendor ecosystem. Motivair HQ in Buffalo, NY. Since 2019 was Cray supercomputer liquid cooling provider at densities up to 400kW/rack. Specific supercomputer customer names not disclosed.

facility_name: Schneider Electric / Motivair --- NVIDIA Reference Design Deployments
opportunity_summary: Schneider Electric collaborates with NVIDIA on reference designs for DGX SuperPods. Prefabricated EcoStruxure Pod Data Centers support pods up to 1MW+. Motivair in-rack liquid cooling with EIA, ORV3 and NVIDIA MGX modular standards. MCDU-45 and MCDU-55 CDUs available globally, production ramping Q1 2026.
notes: Schneider Electric vendor ecosystem. Reference designs co-developed with NVIDIA. Customer-specific deployments not publicly named.

facility_name: Google --- Danfoss Turbocor Cooling & Heat Reuse Partnership

opportunity_summary: Danfoss providing Turbocor compressors and heat reuse modules for Google data centers. Heat recovery supplies renewable energy for on-site heating and neighboring communities. Partnership announced Jan 2024 at AHR Expo. Net Zero Innovation Hub co-founded with Google in Fredericia, Denmark (Sep 2023).
notes: Danfoss vendor ecosystem. Strategic partnership includes AI solutions exchange. Specific DC sites not named publicly. Fredericia used as representative location for the partnership hub.

facility_name: NTT Continental Europe --- Danfoss Turbocor Chiller Deployment
opportunity_summary: NTT Continental Europe uses Danfoss Turbocor exclusively as compressors in installed chillers. Stepless speed control via frequency converters provides significant improvement in part-load efficiency. Deployed for approximately 2 years (as of case study publication).
notes: Danfoss vendor ecosystem. NTT case study. Frankfurt used as representative NTT Continental Europe location.

facility_name: Equinix Amsterdam --- Danfoss VLT Drives Deployment

facility_name: San Diego Wireless Technology Company --- Danfoss Turbocor Chillers
opportunity_summary: Three 1,400-ton ArcticCool chillers powered by 21 Danfoss Turbocor compressors. Over 30% annual savings on compressor energy costs. World-leading wireless technology company (likely Qualcomm given San Diego HQ).
notes: Danfoss vendor ecosystem. Customer not named in case study but location strongly suggests Qualcomm.

opportunity_summary: HPE IT Sustainability Services - Data Center Heat Recovery module. Off-the-shelf product leveraging Danfoss Turbocor compressors. Cools DCs up to 30% more efficiently while recovering and reusing excess heat. Announced Jun 2024.
notes: Danfoss vendor ecosystem. Product partnership, not site-specific deployment. Houston used as HPE HQ representative.

facility_name: ThermalWorks / Edged --- Waterless Cooling with Danfoss Turbocor (Multiple Sites)
opportunity_summary: ThermalWorks deploying integrated waterless cooling with Danfoss Turbocor TGS 230 compressors to 12+ data centers operational or under construction worldwide. Supports up to 70kW/rack air and 200kW/rack liquid. 73% reduction in overhead energy. Won Danfoss EnVisioneer Award 2023 and 2024.
notes: Danfoss vendor ecosystem. ThermalWorks ships to clients across Europe and North America. 12+ DCs confirmed. Individual site names not disclosed.

facility_name: US Hyperscaler --- Munters $215M CRAH Order (Multiple US Sites)

opportunity_summary: Record $215M combined order from single US hyperscaler. Custom-designed CRAHs for air-cooled and hybrid AI data halls. Deliveries at multiple US DCs from end of 2026 through early 2028. Largest combined order from a single Munters customer ever. Booked Q4 2025.

opportunity_summary: 14 Munters Oasis units installed in OPW's 2.4 MW government facility. Stores all data for Irish government policy implementation. Funded by Irish Government and EU under National Recovery and Resilience Plan. Oasis uses up to 80% less energy than conventional cooling.

opportunity_summary: 200,000 sqft expansion at Daleville VA. Consolidates all Virginia production. New chiller test laboratory. Enables local production of Geoclima chiller technology. Products: CW-CRAHs, Fan Coil Arrays, CDUs, SyCool chillers, AHUs. Construction spring 2025, completion Q2 2026.

opportunity_summary: Over 500 StatePoint units deployed globally, cooling 1,000,000+ sqft of DC space. 400% increase in year-on-year demand in Q3 2024 including several hyperscalers. Orders up 500% YoY. New 5MW+ skidded CDU launched Sep 2025 for hyperscale liquid cooling.

cooling_type: Turbocor oil-free centrifugal compressors for DC chillers
opportunity_summary: 140,000+ Turbocor compressors installed worldwide in various data center applications. Oil-free magnetic bearing technology. Latest TGS380 compressor for high-density AI DCs. Serves all 86 Max Planck Institutes.

Site has 230MW electrical infrastructure. 11-country European consortium. One of the world's greenest supercomputers.
notes: Site has 230MW electrical infrastructure for future expansion. Carbon-negative operations due to heat recovery. 11-country consortium. Kajaani Data Center Ecosystem includes multiple facilities.

Key platform for French AI research. Paris-Saclay campus.

cooling_type: Direct liquid cooling (HPE Cray EX closed-loop system). Geothermal cooling solution (CSIRO-pioneered) saves ~7 million litres of water/year. 120kW solar panel array.
opportunity_summary: Fastest supercomputer in Southern Hemisphere. 50 PFLOP/s peak. 217,088 AMD compute cores + 768 GPU cores. 1,792 nodes across 8 HPE Cray EX cabinets. DLC with CSIRO-pioneered geothermal cooling saves 7M litres water/year. #4 Green500 (56.983 GFlops/watt). 120kW solar panels. 30x more powerful than predecessors with only 50% power increase. $48M AUD system.

Reduces data centre power costs by up to 40%. Free cooling leverages Canberra climate (low humidity). Major upgrade in 2024 to support growing AI/HPC demands.
